Items.Title: 宣布 Appwrite OSS 基金 | Announcing the Appwrite OSS Fund
Items.Description:
构建和维护一个开源软件（OSS），这个项目很难。 如果你的项目流行起来，那就更难了。 当我两年前开始构建 Appwrite 时，我爱上了社区、人们和 OSS 方式。 我只花了几天时间就明白生活中没有什么是我想做的。 终于，在项目开始发展的三个月后，我决定辞去我的日常工作，实现我的梦想，成为一名全职的 OSS 维护者。

我几乎不知道这会有多难。 几个月来，我致力于改进项目、提供支持、创建内容和传播信息。 我一直相信 Appwrite 具有巨大的潜力，但与此同时，我知道将需要大量的努力和资源才能使项目达到它可以和需要的地方。

在全职从事该项目 12 个月后，我开始感到财务上的痛苦。 我真的是背对着墙。 对产品的了解还远未完成或处于可以将其货币化的状态，我开始考虑收集 OSS 捐款，提供优质支持，甚至取消我的租金，因为我开始感到超载的费用压力。

在有史以来最好的时机，我从 Ibex 和 Seedcamp 的优秀人士那里获得了一份惊人的投资提议，他们提出支持我对 Appwrite 的愿景。 六个月后，Bessemer（Auth0，Cloudinary）和 Flybridge（Firebase，MongoDB）的投资紧随其后，领投了我们的 850 万种子轮融资。

今天，我们拥有超过 27 名全职 Appwriters 并得到超过 150,000 名开发者社区的支持，我们比以往任何时候都更强大，这意味着是时候回馈和支持那些不如我们幸运的人，并记住我们旅程的起源. 今天是宣布 Appwrite OSS 基金的时候了。

为什么选择 OSS 基金？

开源就是与社区一起协作、学习、分享和建设。 秉承这种精神，我们在 Appwrite 考虑通过引入“Appwrite OSS Fund”来回馈社区。 这是我们回馈社区的方式——这是我们公司的核心——成长和帮助他人成长。 如果阅读 Eldad 的故事让您想到“这听起来很像我”，那么这就是您申请 OSS 基金并将您的项目提升到您梦寐以求的水平的提示。

在将您的梦想项目变为现实的过程中，您所需要的只是构建一个很棒的开源项目的愿景。 该基金背后的想法是支持像您这样的开发人员和您正在构建的开源项目。

基金如何运作？

你从这里申请。

  • 如果选择，您将获得价值2500 美元的基金。
  • 我们通过 GitHub 赞助商或 Open Collective 向您付款。
  • 您可以提名自己或您认识的任何其他维护者。
  • 您的提名数量没有限制 - 每个被提名人​​一个项目除外。
  • 收到您的申请后，将在 15-30 天内回复电子邮件。
  • 为了审核申请，我们将成立一个由 Appwrite 核心团队和 Appwrite 社区代表组成的委员会来评估所有申请。 委员会将根据项目受欢迎程度、技术影响、创新和当前项目的财务能力等多种因素来决定如何最好地利用项目资金。
  • 如需更多信息，请访问此处。

    • 这还不够

    我们将始终想出新的方法来扩大对社区的支持，并确保这将成为我们在 Appwrite 所做的一切的核心，但我们也认识到仅靠我们的支持是不够的。 我们呼吁其他公司和个人加入我们。 以多种可能的方式之一支持项目和维护者，并共同努力创造一个可持续的未来，在这个未来，开源不仅是我们从中受益匪浅的东西，也是我们积极回馈的东西。

    致谢

    我们很荣幸有机会和特权为开发人员的成长做出贡献。 感谢大家对 Appwrite 的喜爱和支持，它给了我们每天变得更好的热情。

    让开源精神永不枯竭——最大限度地分享Appwrite OSS基金，帮助我们触及那些不为人知的声音。



    原文:

    Building and maintaining an Open Source Software(OSS), the project is hard. If your project gets popular, it becomes even harder. When I started building Appwrite two years ago, I fell in love with the community, the people, and the OSS way. It only took me a few days to understand there is nothing in life I want to do more. Finally, three months after the project started gaining momentum, I decided to leave my day job and follow my dream, of becoming a full-time OSS maintainer.

    Little did I know how hard it would be. For months I worked on improving the project, providing support, creating content, and spreading the word. I always believed Appwrite has huge potential, but at the same breath, I knew it was going to require massive efforts and resources to get the project where it can and needs to be.

    After 12 months of working on the project full-time, I started feeling financial pain. I was literally with my back to the wall. Understanding the product is far from complete or in a state where I can monetize it, I started to think about collecting OSS donations, providing premium support, and even as far as canceling my rental as I was starting to feel the pressure of overloading expenses.

    In the best timing ever, I got an amazing investment offer from the wonderful individuals at Ibex and Seedcamp that offered to support my vision for Appwrite. Six months later, they were followed by an investment from Bessemer (Auth0, Cloudinary) and Flybridge (Firebase, MongoDB) that led our 8.5m seed round.

    Today with over 27 full-time Appwriters and backed by a community of over 150,000 developers, we're stronger than ever, which means it's time to give back and support those who weren't as fortunate as us and remember the origins of our journey. Today is the time to announce the Appwrite OSS Fund.

    Why OSS fund?

    Open-source is all about collaborating, learning, sharing, and building together with the community. Keeping up this spirit, we at Appwrite thought of giving back to the community with the introduction to “Appwrite OSS Fund”. This is our way of giving back to the community - which forms the core of our company - growing and helping others grow. If reading Eldad's story made you think to yourself, "that sounds just like me," then this is your cue to apply for the OSS Fund, and take your project to the level you dreamt of.

    In the journey of turning your dream project into a reality, all that you need to have is the vision to build an awesome open-source project. The idea behind the fund is to support developers like you and the open-source project you are building.

    How does the fund work?

    The Appwrite OSS Fund is simple:

    • You apply for it from here.
    • If selected, you get a fund worth $2500.
    • We pay you via GitHub sponsors or Open Collective.
    • You can nominate yourself or any other maintainers you know.
    • There is no bar to the number of nominations you make - with an exception of one project per nominee.
    • Once your application is received, a response email will be within 15-30 days.
    • To review the applications, we will set up a committee composed of the core Appwrite team and representatives of the Appwrite community to assess all applications. The committee will decide how the funding will be best used by applying projects based on multiple factors, ranging from project popularity, technological impact, innovation, and the current project’s financial capabilities.
    • For more information, visit here.

    This Is Not Enough

    We will always think of new ways to expand our support for the community and ensure this will be core to everything we do at Appwrite, but we also recognize our support alone will not be enough. We call other companies and individuals to join us. Support projects and maintainers in one of many ways possible, and work together to create a sustainable future where open source is not only something we get a lot from but also something we are actively giving back to.

    Acknowledgment

    We are humbled to have the opportunity and privilege to contribute to the growth of developers. Thank you for all the love and support that you all have showered on Appwrite, it gives us the enthusiasm to become better every day.

    Let the spirit of open-source never get dull - share about the Appwrite OSS Fund to the maximum, help us reach the unheard voices.

    原文:

    Cuando la aplicación de consola se utiliza en una empresa, probablemente necesitará configurar todas las herramientas para salir a internet por el proxy corporativo.

    Suponiendo que el proxy tiene los siguientes elementos

    • username
    • password
    • host
    • port

    Da como resultado la siguiente configuracion http://username:password@host:port

    Git proxy

    use los siguientes comandos

    git config --global http.proxy http://username:password@host:port
git config --global https.proxy http://username:password@host:port

    o editando directamente el archivo ~/.gitconfig

    [http]
proxy = http://username:password@host:port
[https]
proxy = http://username:password@host:port

    NPM proxy

    npm config set proxy http://username:password@host:port
npm config set https-proxy http://username:password@host:port

    o editando directamente el archivo ~/.npmrc

    proxy=http://username:password@host:port
https-proxy=http://username:password@host:port
https_proxy=http://username:password@host:port

    IONIC proxy

    ionic config set -g proxy http://username:password@host:port
ionic config set -g https-proxy http://username:password@host:port

    Yarn proxy

    yarn config set proxy http://username:password@host:port
yarn config set https-proxy http://username:password@host:port

Items.Title: 在 Snap Toolbox 上使用 Sentinel 1 Imagery 进行洪水测绘 | Flood mapping using Sentinel 1 Imagery on Snap Toolbox
Items.Description:
该数据集可以从Copernicus Open Access Hub下载。

洪水是洪水淹没通常干燥的土地。 这是一种非常常见的灾害类型，主要发生在雨季。 洪水测绘有助于规划和减轻风险。

在本文中，我将使用 Snap Toolbox 上的 sentinel 1 图像提供的 SAR 数据集来介绍洪水映射技术。 洪水测绘的总体方法如下所示，

需要上传方法图片

下载数据并将其加载到 Snap Toolbox

您可以从Copernicus Open Access Hub免费下载数据。 您需要下载洪水事件前后的数据。 比方说，如果洪水事件发生在 7 月 31 日，您需要 7 月 31 日之前和 7 月 31 日之后的哨兵 1 数据集。您可以按如下方式过滤数据，

  • 在你的研究区域上画出边界。
  • 在 Mission Sentinel 1 下，选择卫星平台为S1A_*
  • 选择产品类型为GRD
  • 点击搜索按钮

下载的数据将采用.zip格式。 别担心，Snap Toolbox 可以直接从.zip格式读取数据。

请注意，在应用任何校正之前，数据将以随机方向加载。 我的意思是，地图不会是北向的。

将数据屏蔽到感兴趣区域

要屏蔽数据集，您需要将数据集缩放到您感兴趣的区域。 在raster菜单下，您将找到Subset选项，您可以从中将数据裁剪到所需区域。

要并排查看两个图像，您可能需要view > syncronise image views和Window > Tile evenly 。

多视

这是减小图像大小的过程。 它还可能会减少图像上的一些斑点。 它完全是可选的，如果您需要高分辨率输出，请不要执行此步骤。 多视功能可在此处获得： Radar > SAR Utilities > Multilooking视。

辐射校准

虽然未校准的 SAR 图像足以用于定性使用，但校准的 SAR 图像对于 SAR 数据的定量使用至关重要。 该功能在此处可用： Radar > Radiometric > Calibrate 。

如果您想更好地可视化图像，只需right click on the image > Linear to/from dB 。

地形校正

由于场景的地形变化和卫星传感器的倾斜，SAR 图像中的距离可能会失真。 不直接位于传感器天底位置的图像数据会有一些失真。 地形校正旨在补偿这些失真，使图像的几何表示尽可能接近真实世界。 应用此校正后，图像将正确定向。 您可以将所有参数保留为默认值。 此功能在此处可用： Radar > Geometry > Terrain correction > Range Doppler Terrain Correction 。

创建堆栈

应用辐射和几何校正后，您需要创建合成波段，即将两个图像叠加为一个。 为此，您需要将图像转换为条带并保存产品。 之后，您需要创建堆栈，该工具可在此处获得： Radar > Coregistration > Stack Tools > Create Stack 。

之后，您现在可以可视化产品并查看两张地图之间的差异。 您的最终产品将如下所示，

如果您喜欢这个博客，请订阅我的 YouTube 频道以获取更多与 GIS 和遥感主题相关的内容：https: //www.youtube.com/c/iamtekson

参考

  1. ESA Echoes in Space - Hazard: Flood mapping with Sentinel-1 ESA 空间回声 - 危险：使用 Sentinel-1 进行洪水测绘


原文:

The sentinel 1 imagery is the freely available SAR dataset. The dataset can be downloaded from the Copernicus Open Access Hub.

The snap Toolbox is an open source software for processing the sentinel products. The snap software provides the interactive GUI to process the raster dataset.

A flood is an overflow of water that submerges land that is usually dry. It is very common type of hazard which mainly occur in rainy season. The flood mapping can help to plan and mitigate the risk.

In this article, I am going to write about the flood mapping technique using SAR dataset provided by sentinel 1 imagery on Snap Toolbox. The overall methodology for flood mapping will be looks like this,

Need to upload an methodology image

Download data and load it into Snap Toolbox

You can download the data freely from Copernicus Open Access Hub. You need to download the data before and after flood event. Lets say, if the flood event happen in July 31, you need sentinel 1 dataset before July 31 and after July 31. You can filter the data as below,

  • Draw the boundary on your study area.
  • Under mission sentinel 1, select satellite platform as S1A_*
  • Select product type as GRD
  • Click the search button

The downloaded data will be in .zip format. Don't worry, Snap Toolbox can read the data directly from .zip format.

Please note that before applying any correction, the data will load in random orientation. I mean, the map will not be north oriented.

Mask data to the Area of Interest

To mask the dataset, you need to zoom the dataset to your area of interest. Under raster menu, you will find the Subset option, from where you can crop the data to the required area.

To view two image side by side, you might need to view > syncronise image views and Window > Tile evenly.

Multilooking

It is the process for reducing the size of the image. It might also reduce some spackles on the image. It is totally optional, if you need the high resolution output, don't do this step. The multilooking feature is available here: Radar > SAR Utilities > Multilooking.

Radiometric Calibration

The objective of SAR calibration is to provide imagery in which the pixel values can be directly related to the radar backscatter of the scene. Though uncalibrated SAR imagery is sufficient for qualitative use, calibrated SAR images are essential to quantitative use of SAR data. The feature is available here: Radar > Radiometric > Calibrate.

If you want to visualize the image more nicely, you can convert it to the dB by simply right click on the image > Linear to/from dB.

Terrain correction

Due to topographical variations of a scene and the tilt of the satellite sensor, distances can be distorted in the SAR images. Image data not directly at the sensor’s Nadir location will have some distortion. Terrain corrections are intended to compensate for these distortions so that the geometric representation of the image will be as close as possible to the real world. After applying this correction, the image will be correctly oriented. You can leave all the parameters as default value. This feature is available here: Radar > Geometry > Terrain correction > Range Doppler Terrain Correction.

Create stack

After applying the radiometric and geometric correction, you need to create the composite band, i.e. need to stack the both images into one. To do so, you need to convert the image to band and save the product. After that, you need to create the stack, the tool is available here: Radar > Coregistration > Stack Tools > Create Stack.

After that now you can visualize the products and see the difference between two maps. Your final product will look like this,

If you like this blog, please subscribe to my YouTube channel for more contents related to GIS and Remote Sensing topics: https://www.youtube.com/c/iamtekson

References

  1. ESA Echoes in Space - Hazard: Flood mapping with Sentinel-1

Items.Title: #03：支持 API 的静态 Web 应用程序 | #03: API-Enabling Static Web Apps
Items.Description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 1, Day 3天！

昨天，我们从 GitHub 存储库构建并部署了我们的第一个静态 Web 应用程序（使用 vanillaJS 模板）。 我们使用了 VS Code 扩展，只需单击几下，它就可以透明地为我们设置自动化的 GitHub Actions (CI/CD) 工作流程。 今天 - 我们了解如何使用 Azure Functions 将无服务器 API 端点添加到我们的应用程序，从而帮助我们更有效地根据需求扩展 API。

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 为什么要使用 SWA 实现无服务器？
  • 使用 Azure Functions 添加 API
  • 快速入门：使用托管函数
  • 自带功能
  • 示例：将Azure-SWAG带到 5 月 4 日！
  • 练习：自己试试！

关于作者

这篇文章是与微软 Azure 容器应用、Azure 静态 Web 应用和 Azure Functions 的产品经理Anthony Chu共同撰写的。 关注他@nthonyChu或就在 dev.to 上

为什么选择无服务器？

在介绍 SWA时，我们讨论了如何构建静态 Web 应用程序以将静态内容资产和动态 API 端点分离为可以独立提供和扩展的独立组件。

在昨天的文章中，我们研究了一个只有内容资产（没有 API）的基本静态 Web 应用程序。 使用 SWA 部署它帮助我们利用 Azure 的全球分布的内容服务器实现快速且经济高效的访问。 今天，我们想添加一个 API 端点来支持来自客户端的动态请求。 那么无服务器意味着什么，我们如何实现无服务器 API 端点？

在这种情况下， 无服务器是关于使用事件驱动的架构来按需分配我们的计算资源。 因此，我们的 API 服务器将随着需求的增长而扩展，但在需求下降时具有成本效益。

事件处理是通过在动态分配的计算资源上运行的函数（代码块）实现的。 资源可以随着 API 请求的增长而增加，并随着需求的下降而减少。 使用 Azure 函数

静态 Web 应用程序和 Azure Functions 无缝集成，支持与安全性（用户数据）和路由集成相关的关键功能：

  • 但它会变得更好。 可以通过两种方式为 Azure Functions 配置 SWA：

    • 托管函数- Azure 静态 Web 应用服务在其中配置和管理您的 Azure Functions 部署。
    • 自带功能- 使用预先存在的 Azure Functions 应用程序并自行管理部署。

    免费计划仅支持托管功能选项。 它有一些限制- 例如，仅支持 HTTP 触发器并且缺乏对持久功能等的支持 - 但设置快速且无缝使用。 我们今天将探讨这个选项，但会在第 4 周寻找一篇详细讨论自带功能方法的帖子。

    添加 API

    想要获得一些将 API 端点添加到静态 Web 应用程序部署的实践经验吗？ 查看这个基于您之前部署的 vanilla-JS 应用程序的快速入门教程。 关键步骤是：

    安装适用于 VS Code 的Azure Functions 扩展。

  • 打开您的应用程序根文件夹（“/”） - 创建一个“api”子文件夹
  • 通过命令面板选择“Azure 静态 Web 应用程序：创建 HTTP 函数”或在 Azure 扩展菜单中选择闪电选项。
  • 输入所需的配置信息（语言=“JavaScript”，函数名=“消息”），并确认。

坐下来等待该过程完成设置。 这将使用根文件夹中的配置文件 (*.json) 和包含 2 个文件的/api/message子文件夹填充/api目录：

  • 按照以下步骤在本地开发环境中安装工具并一起运行/测试您的应用程序和 API。 在第 3 周（“开发人员工具”）中查找详细的 2 部分帖子，其中更详细地介绍了 SWA CLI 功能、配置和使用。

    这是确保 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序部署工作流也采用新的 API 功能的关键步骤。

    • 打开 SWA 的默认 GitHub Actions 工作流文件，并将api_location属性更新为“api”。
    • 保存并提交更改以触发重建和部署。

    想查看 API 的实际应用吗？ 向下滚动到“ Exercise部分，我们将介绍一个快速活动，您可以使用 GitHub (SWAG) 构建和部署静态 Web 应用程序，并在此过程中庆祝 #StarWars 日！

    喜欢视频教程？ 查看此Azure 静态 Web 应用程序：提示和技巧，它从react-starter模板介绍了整个过程，并展示了对 Visual Studio Code 的远程开发容器的使用，从而进一步简化了您的开发体验。

    练习：SWAG！

    今天是 5 月 4 日——星球大战日！ 如果我们可以用我们最喜欢的《星球大战》名言来庆祝这一点，那不是很好吗？ 谢天谢地，我的同事 Yohan有一个很棒的Azure-SWAG模板，它将来自Unsplash的图像与 API 函数（“/api/quote”）中的一组预定义引号组合在一起。 在他的原始帖子中阅读它：

    因此，我为5 月 4 日的致敬创建了一个版本。 这是部署时的样子。 每次刷新 SWA 以获取新图像和报价（来自 API）。

    • 查看代码库
    • 查看已部署的 SWA

    你的任务：按照这些步骤复制我的项目，然后探索index.js代码以查看/api/quote函数是如何实现的，并查看app.js代码以查看该 API 在应用程序中是如何被调用的。

    有用的资源



原文:

Welcome to Week 1, Day 3 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

Yesterday, we built and deployed our first Static Web App (using a vanillaJS template) from a GitHub repo. We used the VS Code Extension and, with just a few clicks, it setup automated GitHub Actions (CI/CD) workflows for us transparently. Today - we learn how we can add serverless API endpoints to our application using Azure Functions, helping us scale APIs more effectively with demand.

What We'll Cover

  • Why go Serverless with SWA?
  • Add API with Azure Functions
  • Quickstart: Use Managed Functions
  • Bring Your Own Functions
  • Example: Bring Azure-SWAG to May 4!
  • Exercise: Try it out yourself!

About the Author(s)

This post is co-authored with Anthony Chu, a Product Manager at Microsoft for Azure Container Apps, Azure Static Web Apps and Azure Functions. Follow him @nthonyChu or right here on dev.to

Why Go Serverless?

When Introducing SWA, we talked about how static web apps are architected to separate static content assets and dynamic API endpoints into separate components that can be served and scaled independently.

In yesterday's post we looked at a basic Static Web App with just content assets (no API). Deploying it with SWA helped us take advantage of Azure's globally-distributed content servers for fast and cost-effective access. Today, we want to add an API endpoint to support dynamic requests from clients. So what does serverless mean, and how do we implement serverless API endpoints?

In this context, Serverless is about using an event-driven architecture to allocate our compute resources on-demand. So, our API server will scale as demand grows, but be cost-effective when demand drops.

Azure Functions makes implementing your serverless solutions easier. Event handling is implemented with functions (code blocks) that run on compute resources that are dynamically-allocated. Resources can spin up as API requests grow, and spin down as demand drops. Try this learning path for a deep-dive into using Azure Functions.

Use Azure Functions

Static Web Apps and Azure Functions are integrated seamlessly, enabling key features with respect to security (user data) and routing integrations:

  • User data access - API functions can directly access user authenticaton and role-based authorization data when handling requests.
  • API Route access - the application can access the /api route securely, without requiring custom CORS rules.

But it gets better. Your SWA can be configured for Azure Functions in two ways:

  • Managed Functions - where the Azure Static Web Apps service configures and manages your Azure Functions deployment.
  • Bring Your Own Functions - where you use a pre-existing Azure Functions application and manage the deployment yourself.

The Free plan supports only the Managed Functions option. It has some limitations - e.g., supports only HTTP triggers and lacks support for Durable Functions etc. - but is quick to setup and seamless to use. We'll explore this option today but look for a post in Week 4 that will discuss the Bring Your Own Functions approach in detail.

Adding the API

Want to get some hands-on experience with adding an API endpoint to your Static Web Apps deployment? Check out this Quickstart tutorial that builds on the vanilla-JS application you deployed previously. The key steps are:

1. Setup Azure Functions for SWA

  • Install the Azure Functions Extension for VS Code.
  • Open your application root folder ("/") - create an "api" subfolder
  • Select "Azure Static Web Apps: Create HTTP Function" through Command Palette or select the lightning bolt option in Azure extension menu.
  • Enter required configuration information (language="JavaScript", function name="message"), and confirm.

Sit back and wait till the process completes setup. This populates the /api directory with configuration files (*.json) in the root folder, and a /api/message subfolder that contains 2 files:

  • function.json - defines this function's trigger, bindings and other settings.
  • index.js - implements an async function (accessed at /api/message)

2. Update the index.js file to implement your function - making sure it returns a desired response given the incoming API request and context (input parameters).

3. Update your application code to invoke the API - you can do this by calling fetch('/api/message') directly - the seamless Azure Functions integration ensures that the API routing happens automagically.

4. Test the integration locally with the SWA CLI.
The Azure Static Web Apps CLI (SWA CLI) is a tool to support local development for Static Web Apps by emulating required services where needed. Follow these steps to install the tools and run/test your app and API together in your local development environment. Look for a detailed 2-part post in Week 3 ("Developer Tools") where we cover the SWA CLI capabilities, configuration, and usage, in more detail.

5. Update the GitHub Actions workflow with API location

This is the key step to make sure the Azure Static Web Apps deployment workflow also picks up the new API function.

  • Open the default GitHub Actions workflow file for your SWA and update the api_location property to "api".
  • Save and commit changes to trigger rebuild and deploy.

Want to see API in action? Scroll down to the Exercise section where we walk through a quick activity you can do to build and deploy a Static Web App with GitHub (SWAG) and celebrate #StarWars Day in the process!

Prefer a video tutorial? Check out this Azure Static Web Apps: Tips & Tricks that walks through the process from a react-starter template, and showcases the use of remote development container for Visual Studio Code that further streamlines your development experience.

Exercise: SWAG!

Today is May 4 - Star Wars Day! Wouldn't it be nice if we could celebrate this with our favorite Star Wars quotes? Thankfully, my colleague Yohan has a great Azure-SWAG template that combines images from Unsplash with a pre-defined set of quotes in an API function ("/api/quote"). Read about it in his original post here:

So I created a version for my May 4 homage. Here's what that looks like when deployed. Refresh the SWA to get a new image and quote each time (from the API).

  • See the Code Repository
  • See the Deployed SWA

Your assignment: follow these steps to replicate my project, then explore the index.js code to see how the /api/quote function is implemented and review the app.js code to see how that API is invoked within the application.

Useful Resources

Items.Title: 什么是Nokogiri？ | What is Nokogiri?
Items.Description:
哦……没有帮助？ 继续阅读。

宝石

我首先注意到它是因为它出现在 Bundler 错误中。

让我们开始吧。

使用 Nokogiri 解析 HTML

您想使用 Ruby 从网页中提取数据吗？

首先运行nokogiri -v并查看是否收到带有版本号的响应。 如果没有，您将不得不运行gem i nokogiri

 ➜ ~ irb --simple-prompt >> require 'nokogiri' => true >> Nokogiri => Nokogiri

不要忘记要求它，否则你会得到一个未初始化常量的 NameError 。

首先，我们将从解析我们创建的 HTML 开始，然后我们将解析一个实际的网页。

 # First, put some valid html into a string. >> html = "<h1>Hello World!</h1> <section><h2>A List of Stuff</h2> <ul><li>Camera</li> <li>Computer</li> <li>Television</li> </ul></section>" # Now pass that string into the HTML5 module as an argument >> document = Nokogiri :: HTML5 ( html )

你应该得到这样的回应

=> #(Document:0x4c4 { name = "document" , children = [ #(Element:0x4d8 { name = "html" , children = [ #(Element:0x4ec { name = "head" }), #(Element:0x500 { name = "body" , children = [ #(Element:0x514 { name = "h1" , children = [ #(Text "Hello World!")] }), #(Element:0x528 { name = "section" , children = [ #(Element:0x53c { name = "h2" , children = [ #(Text "A List of Stuff")] }), #(Element:0x550 { name = "ul" , children = [ #(Element:0x564 { name = "li" , children = [ #(Text "Camera")] }), #(Element:0x578 { name = "li" , children = [ #(Text "Computer")] }), #(Element:0x58c { name = "li" , children = [ #(Text "Television")] })] })] })] })] })] })

让我们看一对。 文档#children

这将返回一个类似数组的XML::NodeSet 。 它只有 1 的长度，因为它是我们从未包含在字符串中的<html>...</html>元素。 这个孩子有两个孩子， <head>...</head>和<body>...</body>标签。

 >> body = document . children . children . last => #(Element:0x80c { ... >> ul = body . children . children . children => [ #<Nokogiri::XML::Text:0x8c0 "A List of Stuff">, #<Nokogiri::XML::Elemen... ?> => [ #<Nokogiri::XML::Text:0x8c0 "A List of Stuff">, #<Nokogiri::XML::Elemen.. . > > >> ul . children . length >> => 3 ?> ? > ul . children . each do | child | >> ?> puts child . text >> >> end >> Camera >> Computer >> Television

解析来自 Internet 的 HTML

我听说 Internet 是查找要解析的 HTML 文档的好地方。 您可以使用终端中的客户端 URL 命令来捕获其中的一些 HTML。

像这样捕捉它。

 ➜ ~ curl 'https://ruby-doc.org'

虽然看到 HTML 填充终端很酷，但当它作为 Ruby 对象返回以供我们使用数据时会更好。 为此，让我们使用 Nokogiri！

 # You'll need the open-uri gem in addition to Nokogiri # Open-uri allows you to open a URI as if it was a file in Ruby >> require 'nokogiri' => true >> require 'open-uri' => true >> doc = Nokogiri :: HTML5 ( URI . open ( 'https://ruby-doc.org' )) => #(Document:0x9998 { ... >>

现在我们可以使用#css方法通过元素和类来查询html。

 >> puts doc . css ( 'h1' ) < h1 >< a href = "/" > Ruby - Doc . org < /a></ h1 > => nil >> puts doc . css ( 'p' ). first < p > Help and documentation for the Ruby programming language . < / p >

以这种方式查询类。

 >> rails = Nokogiri :: HTML5 ( URI ( 'https://rubyonrails.org' )) => #(Document:0x1f1bc { ... >> headline = rails . css ( 'div.heading__headline' ) => [ #<Nokogiri::XML::Element:0x1fb94 name="div" attributes=[#<Nokogiri::XML... >> puts headline . text Compress the complexity of modern web apps . Learn just what you need to get started , then keep leveling up as you go . Ruby on Rails scales from HELLO WORLD to IPO . You ' re in good company . Over the past two decades , Rails has taken countless companies to millions of users and billions in market valuations . Building it together . Over six thousand people have contributed code to Rails , and many more have served the community through evangelism , documentation , and bug reports . Join us! Everything you need . Rails is a full - stack framework . It ships with all the tools needed to build amazing web apps on both the front and back end . Optimized for happiness . Let ' s get started .

伟大的！ 现在您对 Nokogiri 的工作原理有了大致的了解！

现在免费奖励内容...

让我们搜索网页的内容。 例如，在撰写本文时是篮球赛季，所以让我们看看 ESPN 的主页是否谈论篮球。

 >> espn = Nokogiri :: HTML5 ( URI ( 'https://www.espn.com' )) => #(Document:0x259e0 { ... # For this we will use a Regular Expression (RegEx) # The Regular Expression goes between forward slashes # And the 'i' flag makes the search case-insensitive >> espn . text . scan ( /basketball/i ). count => 32

你有它！ 而且我们甚至不必访问该网站就可以发现 ESPN 在其主页上 32 次提及篮球！ 哇...



原文:

A Nokogiri is a Japanese pull saw. Oh... didn't help? Keep reading.

The Gem

Nokogiri comes standard the Gemfiles of Rails apps. I first noticed it because it showed up in a Bundler error.

Nokogiri is a low-dependency module written in C, Java, and Ruby that parses XML and HTML so that these protocols can be used by Ruby. Let's get started.

Using Nokogiri to Parse HTML

Do you want to use Ruby to extract data from a webpage?

First run nokogiri -v and see if you get a response with a verion number. If not, you'll have to run gem i nokogiri

➜  ~ irb --simple-prompt
>> require 'nokogiri'
=> true
>> Nokogiri
=> Nokogiri

Don't forget to require it, otherwise you'll get a NameError for an uninitialized constant.

First, we will start by parsing HTML we create, then we will parse an actual webpage.

# First, put some valid html into a string.
>> html = "<h1>Hello World!</h1>
<section><h2>A List of Stuff</h2>
<ul><li>Camera</li>
<li>Computer</li>
<li>Television</li>
</ul></section>"
# Now pass that string into the HTML5 module as an argument
>> document = Nokogiri::HTML5(html)

You should get a response like this

=>
#(Document:0x4c4 { name = "document", children = [ #(Element:0x4d8 { name = "html", children = [ #(Element:0x4ec { name = "head" }), #(Element:0x500 { name = "body", children = [ #(Element:0x514 { name = "h1", children = [ #(Text "Hello World!")] }), #(Element:0x528 { name = "section", children = [ #(Element:0x53c { name = "h2", children = [ #(Text "A List of Stuff")] }), #(Element:0x550 { name = "ul", children = [ #(Element:0x564 { name = "li", children = [ #(Text "Camera")] }), #(Element:0x578 { name = "li", children = [ #(Text "Computer")] }), #(Element:0x58c { name = "li", children = [ #(Text "Television")] })] })] })] })] })] })

Nokogiri's HTML5 module took our HTML string and returned a Document class to us that has it's own set of methods. Let's look at a couple. (Remember you can always list all the methods by using Document.methods.sort.)

Document#children

This will will return an array-like XML::NodeSet. It only has a length of one because it's the <html>...</html> element that we never included in our string. This child has two children, the <head>...</head> and the <body>...</body> tags.

>> body = document.children.children.last
=>
#(Element:0x80c {
...
>> ul = body.children.children.children
=> [#<Nokogiri::XML::Text:0x8c0 "A List of Stuff">, #<Nokogiri::XML::Elemen...
?> => [#<Nokogiri::XML::Text:0x8c0 "A List of Stuff">, #<Nokogiri::XML::Elemen..
.
>> >> ul.children.length
>> => 3
?> ?> ul.children.each do |child|
>> ?> puts child.text
>> >> end
>> Camera
>> Computer
>> Television

Parsing HTML from the Internet

I've heard the Internet is a good place to find HTML documents to parse. You can capture some of this HTML by using the Client URL command in your terminal.

Ruby-Doc.org is a great resource to learn about all Ruby objects.

Capture it like this.

➜  ~ curl 'https://ruby-doc.org'

While it's cool to see the HTML populate the terminal, it's even better when it comes back as a Ruby object for us to use the data. To do so, let's use Nokogiri!

# You'll need the open-uri gem in addition to Nokogiri
# Open-uri allows you to open a URI as if it was a file in Ruby >> require 'nokogiri'
=> true
>> require 'open-uri'
=> true >> doc = Nokogiri::HTML5(URI.open('https://ruby-doc.org'))
=>
#(Document:0x9998 {
...
>>

Now we can use the #css method to query the html by elements and classes.

>> puts doc.css('h1')
<h1><a href="/">Ruby-Doc.org</a></h1>
=> nil
>> puts doc.css('p').first
<p>Help and documentation for the Ruby programming language.</p>

Query class this way.

>> rails = Nokogiri::HTML5(URI('https://rubyonrails.org'))
=>
#(Document:0x1f1bc {
...
>> headline = rails.css('div.heading__headline')
=> [#<Nokogiri::XML::Element:0x1fb94 name="div" attributes=[#<Nokogiri::XML...
>> puts headline.text Compress the complexity of modern web apps. Learn just what you need to get started, then keep leveling up as you go. Ruby on Rails scales from HELLO WORLD to IPO. You’re in good company. Over the past two decades, Rails has taken countless companies to millions of users and billions in market valuations. Building it together. Over six thousand people have contributed code to Rails, and many more have served the community through evangelism, documentation, and bug reports. Join us! Everything you need. Rails is a full-stack framework. It ships with all the tools needed to build amazing web apps on both the front and back end. Optimized for happiness. Let’s get started.

Great! Now you have a general idea of how Nokogiri works!

And now for free bonus content...

Let's search a webpage's content. For instance, it's basketball season at the time of this writing, so let's see if the homepage to ESPN talks about basketball.

>> espn = Nokogiri::HTML5(URI('https://www.espn.com'))
=>
#(Document:0x259e0 {
...
# For this we will use a Regular Expression (RegEx)
# The Regular Expression goes between forward slashes
# And the 'i' flag makes the search case-insensitive
>> espn.text.scan(/basketball/i).count
=> 32

There you have it! And we didn't even have to visit the website to find out that ESPN refers to basketball 32 times on it's homepage! Wow...

Items.Title: 使用 Wasm 的视频到 gif 转换器 | Video to gif converter using Wasm
Items.Description:
欢迎来到另一个博客。
这将是超级令人兴奋的！

今天，我们将使用wasm创建Video to .gif 转换器。
这可以用作您的 wasm 项目或迷你项目。

但是 wasm 允许我们直接在浏览器上执行 CPU 密集型工作。

在这里，我们将使用FFmpeg库（用 c 语言编写）将视频转换为 gif。 我们将 ffmpeg 的 wasm 二进制文件直接下载到一个 react 应用程序中，这可以使我们能够直接通过浏览器将视频编辑工作卸载到我们的系统中。

所以，让我们开始吧。

一种。 创建一个新目录wasm-gif 。
湾。 移动到目录cd wasm-gif/

它将自动为 react 应用程序获取文件。

此命令将让默认反应应用程序在浏览器中运行。

如果我们检查浏览器的元素/开发人员工具中的 Sources 选项卡，我们可以看到代码转换为 WebAssembly 文本。

一种。 转到 src/ 并打开 App.jsx 并删除样板。
湾。 添加

一种。 第一步是为组件添加额外的状态，在这种情况下，一个视频文件最初将在 html 中未定义我们可以添加一个具有文件类型的输入元素，当这个输入发生变化时，我们将运行一个事件处理程序来设置视频状态，它从事件发出文件列表的事件中获取实际文件，然后我们使用项目 0（文件对象）从该列表中选择第一个文件。

 function App() { const [ready, setReady] = useState(false); const [video, setVideo] = useState(); const [gif, setGif] = useState(); const load = async () => { await ffmpeg.load(); setReady(true); } useEffect(() => { load(); }, [])

湾。 现在我们可以访问一个视频文件，我们希望在 html 视频元素中显示它，我们将使用逻辑和运算符仅在定义视频文件时显示视频该元素的控件宽度为 250，但这是一件棘手的事情这里是我们不能只将原始文件传递给 source 属性，我们需要将其转换为浏览器可以获取的 url，因此将文件分配给 URL。



原文:

Welcome to the another Blog.
This one is going to be Super exciting!

Today, we're going to create Video to .gif convertor using wasm.
This could be used as your wasm projects or a mini project.

WebAssembly has made things so easy as traditionally we use to upload our apps to cloud server, where the server was working at the back end. But wasm allow us to perform CPU intensive jobs directly on our browser.

Here, we will use FFmpeg library(written in c language) to convert a video to gif. We will download wasm binary for ffmpeg directly into a react app which can enable us to offload the video editing work into our system directly through the browser.

So, Lets get Started.

1. Creating a Directory

a. Create a new directory wasm-gif.
b. Move into the directory cd wasm-gif/

2. Generating a new react app using snowpack: 

npx create-snowpack-app gifmakr --template @snowpack/app-template-react

It will automatically fetch files for the react app.

3. Install FFmpeg 

npm install @ffmpeg/ffmpeg @ffmpeg/core

4. Run the App

This command will let the default react app run in the browser.

If we check the Sources tab in elements/developer tools of the Browser, we can see the code converted into WebAssembly Text.

5. Writing the react code.

a. Go to src/ and open App.jsx and remove the boilerplate.
b. Add

(i) working with this library we'll set the log option to true so we can see everything that it does directly in the console now it's important to understand at this point that the actual webassembly binary has not been bundled in our application it's a pretty large file so we don't want it to block our web application right away.

import { createFFmpeg, fetchFile } from '@ffmpeg/ffmpeg';
const ffmpeg = createFFmpeg({ log: true });

(ii) To keep track of the loading state we'll go ahead and add a stateful property here called ready with the use state hook it has a default value of false and then we'll create an async function here called load that can flip it to true after the binary has been loaded.

function App() {
  const [ready, setReady] = useState(false);

  const load = async () => {
    await ffmpeg.load();
    setReady(true);
  }

(iii) Now we just need a place to call this function in react, So we can do that with the use effect hook to run this function when the component is first initialized use effect takes a function as its argument then notice how i'm adding an empty array as the second argument this will ensure that the function is only called once when the component is first initialized or mounted then inside the effect we can simply call our load function.

 useEffect(() => {
    load();
  }, [])


  return ready ? () : ( <p> Loading...</p> );
}

export default App;

6. Loading the Video file into the application

a. The first step is to add additional state to the component in this case a video file which will initially start undefined in the html we can add an input element with a type of file when this input changes we'll run an event handler that will set the video state and it gets the actual file from the event the event emits a file list and then we use item 0(FILE Object) to select the first file from that list.

function App() {
  const [ready, setReady] = useState(false);
  const [video, setVideo] = useState();
  const [gif, setGif] = useState();

  const load = async () => {
    await ffmpeg.load();
    setReady(true);
  }

  useEffect(() => {
    load();
  }, [])

b. Now that we have access to a video file we want to display it in an html video element we'll use a logical and operator to only show the video when the video file is defined the element has controls a width of 250 but a tricky thing here is that we can't just pass the raw file to the source attribute we need to convert it to a url that the browser can fetch therefore assign a file to the URL.

Items.Title: 如何在 Python 中制作 PDF 文本转语音阅读器 | How to Make a PDF Text-to-Speech Reader in Python
Items.Description:
然而，许多读者更喜欢听他们的内容而不是阅读内容。 幸运的是，您可以使用 Python 将任何 PDF 转换为可以在多种流行设备上播放的音频文件。

使用 Python 创建 PDF 阅读器的第一步是下载和安装正确的库。 在本教程中，我们将使用 PyPDF2 和 pyttsx3。 相关：如何使用 Python 下载 YouTube 视频？

这些库默认是没有安装的，所以我们需要使用pip来安装。 我们可以通过打开命令提示符或终端并输入以下命令来做到这一点：

 pip install PyPDF2 pyttsx3

在 Python 中读取文件很简单。 我们需要使用open()函数打开文件。

然而，要阅读 PDF 的内容，我们需要安装 PyPDF2 库。 我们可以通过创建一个 PdfFileReader 对象并将 PDF 文件的文件路径传递给它来做到这一点。

 from PyPDF2 import PdfFileReader # create a PdfFileReader object reader = PdfFileReader ( "/path/to/file.pdf" )

一个 PDF 文件可能包含多个页面。 所以我们需要指定我们要阅读的页面。

我们可以使用numPages属性找到 PDF 中的页数。 如果您将大型 PDF 转换为有声读物，您可以使用它来循环浏览页面。

 for page in reader . pages : # do the rest #or for i in range ( reader . numPages ): page = reader . pages [ i ] #do the rest

一旦我们决定要阅读哪个页面，我们就需要从该页面中提取文本内容。 在 PyPDF2 中，我们可以使用extractText属性。

 text = page . extractText ()

现在我们有了 PDF 的文本内容，我们需要将其转换为音频。 为此，我们将使用 pyttsx3 库。

它可以离线工作，并且兼容多种平台，包括 Windows、Linux 和 MacOSX。

第一步是创建 pyttsx3 的 Engine 类的实例。

 import pyttsx3 engine = pyttsx3 . init ()

引擎准备好后，我们可以设置声音（男、女）、音量和语速。 但这些都是可选的。

设置声音——男/女

我们需要获取引擎中可用的内容来设置声音。

 voices = engine . getProperty ( 'voices' )

现在，我们可以在引擎上设置语音属性。 使用 0 设置男声，使用 1 设置女声。

 #Male engine . setProperty ( 'voice' , voices [ 0 ]. id ) #Female engine . setProperty ( 'voice' , voices [ 1 ]. id )

设置阅读速度

我们可以通过将 rate 属性设置为您想要的每分钟字数来更改阅读速度。

改变音量

在 pyttsx3 中，可以将音量设置在 0 到 1 之间。0 为静音，1 为最大音量。

 engine . setProperty ( 'volume' ,. 75 )

我们已经完成了所有的后台工作。 现在让我们开始行动。 在 pyttsx3 中，你必须调用say方法，然后调用runAndWait方法来进行实际的语音。

 engine . say ( text ) engine . runAndWait ()

最后，我们可以使用 pyttsx3 的 Engine 类的save_to_file()方法将音频保存为 MP3 格式。

 engine . save_to_file ( text , 'test.mp3' )

将所有内容放在一起我们逐行浏览代码以更好地理解它。 现在，转换 PDF 并将其保存到文件的更完整版本如下所示。

 import pyttsx3 from PyPDF2 import PdfFileReader # create a PdfFileReader object reader = PdfFileReader ( "/path/to/file.pdf" ) # extract text from page 1 (index 0) page = reader . pages [ 0 ] text = page . extractText () # Create a pyttsx3 engine engine = pyttsx3 . init () # set the voice voices = engine . getProperty ( 'voices' ) engine . setProperty ( 'voice' , voices [ 1 ]. id ) #Female # set the speed/rate of speech engine . setProperty ( 'rate' , 150 ) # set the volume engine . setProperty ( 'volume' ,. 75 ) engine . save_to_file ( text , 'test.mp3' )

最后的想法

但读者的偏好也发生了巨大变化。

比起阅读，更多的人想听。 因为在聆听的同时可以做其他事情。 我们在阅读时没有这种灵活性。 此外，听力对视力障碍的人来说更方便。

但是我们没有找到我们在互联网上看到的每个 PDF 资源的音频版本。 但是 Python 程序员只需几行编码就可以轻松地将它们转换为音频文件。

在本文中，我们讨论了如何将 PDF 转换为音频文件。 我们还寻找了用不同的音量、声音和速度来修改语音的方法。

皮埃尔-亨利·索里亚✨ - 詹姆斯'但丁'米兹 - 博主和程序员 - Apr 12 4月12日



原文:

PDFs are great for distribution because we can read them on any PDF reader device. Many readers, however, prefer to listen to their content than read it. Fortunately, you can use Python to turn any PDF into an audio file that we can play on several popular devices.

The first step in creating a PDF reader with Python is downloading and installing the correct libraries. In this tutorial, we’ll be using PyPDF2 and pyttsx3. PyPDF2 is a library to read and manipulate PDF files, while pyttsx3 will convert them into audio files.

Relate: How to Download YouTube Videos With Python?

These libraries are not installed by default, so we need to install them using pip. We can do this by opening a command prompt or terminal and entering the following command:

pip install PyPDF2 pyttsx3

1. Read PDF file using Python

Reading files in Python is straightforward. We need to open the file using the open() function.

Yet, to read the contents of a PDF, we need to install the PyPDF2 library. We can do this by creating a PdfFileReader object and passing the file path of the PDF file to it.

from PyPDF2 import PdfFileReader # create a PdfFileReader object
reader = PdfFileReader("/path/to/file.pdf")

2. Get the page to read

A PDF file may contain multiple pages. So we need to specify which page we want to read.

The PyPDF2 module offers two ways to work with pages. We can find the number of pages in a PDF using numPages property. The pages property returns a list of all the pages in the PDF.

number_of_pages = reader.numPages
page = reader.pages[0]

You could use it to loop through the pages if you're converting a large PDF into an audiobook.

for page in reader.pages: # do the rest

#or
for i in range(reader.numPages): page = reader.pages[i] #do the rest

3. Convert PDF to text using Python

Once we decide which page we will read, we need to extract the text content from that page. In PyPDF2, we can use the extractText property.

text = page.extractText()

4. Configure the pyttsx3 engine

Now that we have the text content of our PDF, we need to convert it into audio. For this, we'll use the pyttsx3 library.

Pyttsx3 is a text-to-speech conversion library in Python. It works offline and is compatible with multiple platforms, including Windows, Linux, and MacOSX.

The first step is to create an instance of the pyttsx3's Engine class.

import pyttsx3
engine = pyttsx3.init()

Once the engine is ready, we can set the voice (male, female), volume, and speaking rate. But these are all optional.

Set the voice—male/female

We need to get what's available in the engine to set the voice.

voices = engine.getProperty('voices')

Now, we can set the voice property on the engine. Use 0 to set the male voice and 1 for the female voice.

#Male
engine.setProperty('voice', voices[0].id) #Female
engine.setProperty('voice', voices[1].id)

Set reading speed

We can change the reading speed by setting the rate property to your desired words per minute.

engine.setProperty('rate', 150) Instead of setting a specific rate, if you only want to speed (or slow) the reading, you can refer to the current speed and adjust it.

# refer to the current value
rate = engine.getProperty('rate') engine.setProperty('rate', rate+50)

Change the volume

In pyttsx3, you can set the volume between 0 and 1. 0 to mute, and 1 to set the volume to its maximum.

engine.setProperty('volume',.75)

5. Read or Save the audio

We've done all the background work. Now let's dive into action. In pyttsx3, you have to call the say method and then the runAndWait method to do the actual speech.

engine.say(text)
engine.runAndWait()

Finally, we can save the audio in MP3 format using the save_to_file() method of pyttsx3's Engine class.

engine.save_to_file(text, 'test.mp3')

Putting it all together We've walked through the code line by line to understand it better. Now, the fuller version to convert PDFs and save them to a file would look like this.

import pyttsx3
from PyPDF2 import PdfFileReader # create a PdfFileReader object
reader = PdfFileReader("/path/to/file.pdf") # extract text from page 1 (index 0)
page = reader.pages[0]
text = page.extractText() # Create a pyttsx3 engine
engine = pyttsx3.init() # set the voice
voices = engine.getProperty('voices')
engine.setProperty('voice', voices[1].id) #Female
 # set the speed/rate of speech
engine.setProperty('rate', 150) # set the volume
engine.setProperty('volume',.75) engine.save_to_file(text, 'test.mp3')

Final thoughts

PDFs are everywhere. But readers’ preferences also change dramatically.

More people want to listen than reading. Because it’s possible to do something else while listening. We don’t get this flexibility when reading. Also, listening is more convenient for people with vision impairment.

But we don’t find audio versions for every PDF resource we see on the internet. But Python programmers would easily convert them into audio files with a few lines of coding.

In this article, we’ve discussed how to convert PDFs into audio files. We’ve also looked for ways to modify the speech with different volumes, voices, and speeds.

Pierre-Henry Soria ✨ - Apr 8

James 'Dante' Midzi - Apr 21

Blogger and Programmer - Apr 12

Items.Title: 何时构建工程工具以及何时购买它们 | When To Build Engineering Tools And When To Buy Them
Items.Description:
许多工程领导者经常考虑的问题之一是在构建产品的过程中是否或何时构建或购买软件。 公司投入大量时间和资源来构建软件，而以其他方式购买软件也可能很昂贵。 在她的演讲中，Nallapeta 描述了她在做出构建与购买之间的决定时考虑的“7 C”，并分享了她在开发 Apartment List 时实施此过程的经验。

能力

第一个 C 是能力，它取决于您要解决的问题，包括您需要或想要构建的任何功能。 这是产品和业务的核心，通常也是使其在其他产品中脱颖而出的原因。 这些功能是您想要进入市场以及您的客户将使用的功能。 那么问题就变成了这些能力是否已经存在。 如果是这样，开发人员需要决定是否值得从头开始构建它们或使用已经可用的工具，这取决于产品需要多快进入市场以及哪条路径有助于最有效地实现目标并且高效。

成本

大多数人在构建时会考虑两个常见的成本维度。 首先是资源，包括一个团队可能需要多少工程师来开发产品。 第二个是时间，考虑到这些工程师在现有资源的情况下构建产品需要多长时间。 大多数团队会考虑两者并选择更便宜的选项。 然而，还有其他相关成本可能会影响您的决策：构建时间、维护成本（监控产品、支持产品、构建测试用例等）、基础设施成本和托管成本（这些成本是隐藏的，但可以加起来很多）。

如果您选择购买，还有其他费用。 转移您已有的解决方案或集成新工具可能会很耗时，而且迁移成本很高。 购买软件还涉及入职成本（工程师学习新系统并适应新系统需要多长时间）、离职成本（通常新旧系统之间存在重叠）和成功长期集成的维护成本。 在根据成本做出决定之前，团队必须超越这两个维度进行思考。

复杂

为了构建易于理解并向包括销售团队、增长团队和所有业务利益相关者在内的其他人解释的系统。 团队经常构建非常复杂的产品，而且随着产品的扩展，复杂性也会增加。 例如，Netflix 的规模如此之大，以至于他们需要投资自己的内容分发网络 (CDN) 并构建 Open Connect，他们可以将其发送到所有 ISP 以在客户端缓存视频以快速交付内容。

权限

产品的构建速度在很大程度上取决于团队的能力。 我们团队中是否有合适的工程师了解需要构建的内容？ 他们是否拥有从业务和客户角度了解我们正在解决的问题的经验和领域知识？ 他们一起工作好吗？ 如果没有，雇佣和建立这个团队需要多长时间？ 建立一个称职的团队可能需要 3-6 个月以上的时间来招聘。
在之前的一家公司，Nallapeta 的团队收购了 10 多家公司，每一次收购都会在几个月内立即增加价值。 如果他们建造，添加这些功能可能需要几个月或更长时间。

凝聚

在购买的情况下，凝聚力或连通性是棘手的——您购买的功能是否与您已经拥有的其他系统集成。 例如，Apartment List 使用 Salesforce 进行客户关系管理 (CRM)，他们构建的任何其他获取数据的软件都应该支持向 Salesforce 发送数据。 您可以通过软件生态系统创造价值，其中系统相互共存并相互依赖。

竞争优势

任何产品在进入市场后都需要能够竞争。 竞争优势是决定产品或其功能是否是其功能和业务的核心的关键因素。 产品的能力决定了它是否具有竞争力，是否可以更快地发布功能以赢得行业。

连续性

连续性与维护密切相关。 它决定了随着用户群的增长和新功能请求的增加，您如何确保产品的可扩展性。 跟踪这一点很重要，这样您就可以保证您的系统是可持续的，并且您不需要每隔几年更换一次系统，这可能是一个重大挫折。

公寓清单的经验

构建：A/B 测试

在构建满足所有功能需求的产品时，开箱即用 (OOTB) 解决方案不足以满足当时的业务需求。 该团队必须快速发布许多实验，并尝试各种基于订阅的模型和付费墙，每个模型的价格和功能都有变化，如果没有快速迭代，这是不可能实现的。

他们还希望保持对定制的控制，以构建出色的用户体验。 该产品针对各种客户和客户子群体进行了高度定制。 它还凭借洞察力和更快的功能发布以及更少的实验和测试设置提供了巨大的竞争优势，从而导致全球客户的增加。

构建：计费系统

在高风险行业，退款率非常高。 使用基于订阅的商业模式，Apartment List 需要维护回退用例，这样如果客户没有付款，系统会自动重定向到不同的支付处理器，这是其他计费系统工具不具备的功能。 这使他们还可以降低续订率并为客户提供更多折扣。 最重要的是，Apartment List 的利润很低，这意味着使用 Stripe 之类的工具会导致他们削减利润。

购买：电子邮件软件

在启动阶段，构建软件并访问定制的复杂功能更有意义。 然而，工程团队没有足够的带宽或文档来支持系统，并且随着时间的推移，随着构建软件的团队离开公司，内部没有足够的软件知识来维护它。 最终使用内部软件也意味着像使用新徽标和颜色进行品牌更新这样简单的事情需要数周或数月的工程努力。

这也帮助公司实现了凝聚力，新系统与现有系统以及彼此很好地集成。

购买：测试软件

每家公司都需要某种形式的质量保证 (QA)，随着产品规模的扩大，这可能成为一个昂贵且乏味的过程，迫使工程师将注意力从产品上转移开。 使用外部工具可以更好地控制和易于使用测试软件，并提高测试过程的效率。

构建和购买：分析

数据是任何公司的支柱，需要高数据准确性和覆盖率。 做出的每一个决定都需要建立在坚实的基础之上。 随着业务需求的不断变化，ApartmentList 选择购买分析软件并在此基础上构建以缩小差距。

了解更多并加入

如果您想了解更多关于 Sue Nallapeta 的决策过程和构建与购买的经验，请查看 YouTube 上的谈话录音： https ://youtu.be/dTSFDkWM1oM

Hacking the Engineering Process hosts monthly meetups. Hacking the Engineering Process 每月举行一次聚会。



原文:

Hacking the Engineering Process is a San Francisco-based meetup, which opens discussion for software engineers and tech enthusiasts to share their thoughts on leading topics, pose thought-provoking questions for each other, and learn more about how various engineering teams measure developer productivity amongst themselves.

In March, the guest speaker was the VP of Engineering at Apartment List, Sue Nallapeta. Nallapeta is a leader in tech and is passionate about building highly performant and vastly scalable engineering systems. One of the questions that many engineering leaders often contemplate is whether or when to build or buy software in the process of building a product. Companies invest a lot of time and resources into building software, while buying software can also be expensive in other ways. In her talk, Nallapeta described the “7 C’s” she considers when making this decision between build vs buy and shared her experience in implementing this process while developing Apartment List.

Capability

The first C is Capability, which is determined by the problem that you are solving for, including any features you need or want to build. This is a product and a business’s core, and often what makes it stand out amongst others. These capabilities are what you want to go to market with and what your customers will use. The question then becomes whether these capabilities already exist. If so, developers need to decide whether it is worth building them from scratch or using the tools that are already available, which is a decision that depends on how quickly the product needs to go to market and which path would help achieve the goal most effectively and efficiently.

Cost

There are two common dimensions to cost that most people will look at when building. The first is resources, including how many engineers a team may need to develop the product. The second being time, on how long it will take for those engineers to build the product given the resources they have available. Most teams will consider the two and choose the cheaper option. There are, however, other pertinent costs that can and possibly should impact your decision: build time, maintenance costs (to monitor the product, support it, build test cases, etc.), infrastructure costs, and hosting costs (which are hidden but can add up to a lot).

There are other costs if you choose to buy. Transferring a solution you already have or integrating a new tool can be time consuming and have major migration costs. Buying software also involves onboarding costs (how long it takes for engineers to learn the new system and become comfortable with it) with offboarding costs (typically there is an overlap between old and new systems) and maintenance costs for successful long-term integration. It is crucial for teams to think beyond these two dimensions before making a decision based on cost.

Complexity

In order to build systems that are easy to understand and explain to others including the sales team, growth team, and all business stakeholders. Teams often build products that are very complex, and complexity also grows as the product scales. For example, Netflix’s scale was so large that they needed to invest in their own Content Distribution Network (CDN) and built Open Connect, which they can ship to all ISPs to cache videos on the client side to deliver content quickly.

Competence

A product’s build speed heavily depends on the team’s competence. Do we have the right engineers on the team who understand what needs to be built? Do they have the experience and domain knowledge to understand what we are solving from the business and customer perspective? Do they work well together? If not, how long will it take to hire and build this team? Building a competent team can take upwards of 3-6 months just to hire.
At a previous company, Nallapeta’s team acquired over 10 companies, each acquisition immediately adding value in the span of a couple of months. If they built, it would have taken several months or more to add those capabilities.

Cohesion

Cohesion, or connectedness, is tricky in the case of buying - whether the feature you are buying integrates with the other systems that you already have. For example, Apartment List uses Salesforce for Customer Relationship Management (CRM), and any other software they build that ingests data should support sending data to Salesforce. You can create value through an ecosystem of softwares, where systems coexist with each other and are dependent on each other.

Competitive Advantage

Any product needs to be able to compete once it enters the market. Competitive advantage is a key factor decided by whether the product or its features are core to its functionality and to the business. The product’s capability determines if it can be competitive and can ship features faster to win the industry.

Continuity

Continuity goes hand-in-hand with maintenance. It determines how you ensure scalability of the product as your user base grows and as new feature requests rise. This is important to track so you can guarantee that your system is sustainable and you don’t need to replace the system every few years, which can be a major setback.

Apartment List’s Experience

Build: A/B testing

In building a product that satisfied all of the capability needs, Out of the Box (OOTB) solutions were insufficient for the business need at the time. Apartment List is a subscription based business. The team had to quickly ship many experiments, and try various subscription based models and paywalls, each with changes in prices and features, which would not have been possible without fast iteration.

They also wanted to maintain control over customization to build a great user experience. The product was highly customized for various customers and customers sub-groups. It also provided a great competitive advantage with insights and quicker feature launches with a lower experimentation and test setup, leading to an increase in customers globally.

Build: Billing System

In a high risk industry, the chargeback rates are extremely high. With a subscription based business model, Apartment List needed to maintain fall back use cases such that if a payment does not go through for a customer, the system automatically redirects to a different payment processor, a feature unavailable in other billing system tools. This allowed them to also lower the renewal rates and offer more discounts to their customers. Most importantly, Apartment List’s margin was low, meaning using a tool like Stripe would lead to them cutting into their margins.

Buy: Email Software

Apartment List had initially built their own email software, which was relevant for the stage of the business. At the startup stage, it made more sense to build the software and have access to customized complex features. However, engineering teams did not have the bandwidth or documentation to support the system and over time, as the team who built the software left the company, there was not enough in-house knowledge about the software to maintain it. Eventually using the in-house software also meant that something as simple as a brand update with new logos and colors would require weeks or months of engineering effort.

Apartment List chose Courier to support transactional emails and another provider for marketing emails. This also helped the company achieve cohesion, with the new systems integrating well with the existing system and each other.

Buy: Testing Software

Every company requires some form of Quality Assurance (QA), which, as the product scales, can become an expensive and tedious process that forces engineers to divide their attention away from the product. Using an external tool offers better control and ease of use of the testing software and increases the efficiency of the testing process.

Build & Buy: Analytics

Data is the backbone of any company, requiring high data accuracy and coverage. Every decision made needs to be built on a strong foundation. With evolving business needs, ApartmentList chose to both buy analytics software and build on top of it to close the gaps.

Learn More & Join

If you would like to learn more about Sue Nallapeta’s decision making process and experience with Build vs Buy, check out the talk recording on YouTube: https://youtu.be/dTSFDkWM1oM

Items.Title: 图像学 | Iconography
Items.Description:
什么是 HTML 万物？

该播客涉及 Web 开发主题以及经营小型企业、个体经营和时间管理。 您可以加入他们的成功和他们的斗争，因为他们试图管理扩展他们的 Web 开发业务，而不会把自己拉得太瘦。

这是关于什么的？

本周，马特和迈克在一个通过图标用户体验涵盖基础知识的紧凑集中解决了“什么是图像学”和“图像学是什么意思”的问题。 二人组还涵盖了各种图像学示例，以及何时应该使用文本而不是图标，或者何时应该一起使用文本和图标以及为什么。

显示注释

图像学用户体验

  • 图标是简单的视觉元素，可以立即识别和理解。”
  • 图标是通用的（它们不依赖于特定的书面/口头语言）
    • 一般来说这是真的，很多人有不同的生活经历，可能不熟悉你试图代表的某种对象
  • 图标是视觉隐喻
    • 通过转移人们已经理解的东西的属性来加速理解
    • 图标向观众传达一种隐喻的联想（即邮件的信封）
    • 比喻可以是……
      • 文字（即闹钟图标是闹钟）
      • 过时（即保存图标是软盘）
      • 摘要（即 shuffle 和 refresh 是箭头）
      • 原始参考丢失（即 Mac 命令图标）
  • 视觉上
    • 代表物体、动作或想法的小符号
    • 通常是正方形的 1:1 纵横比
    • 如果您确实需要使用自定义图标，它们应该共享：
      • 共享主题风格和一致的设计元素
      • 图标应该看起来像是在一个集合或系列中
    • 通过让图标在视觉上保持一致，它们变成：
      • 更美观
      • 消除歧义
      • 帮助用户区分图标是否是交互式的
    • 具有一致外观的图标包括具有相同高度和填充的图标（不要混合填充和非填充图标）。 这可以防止将用户的眼睛不成比例地拉向特定的图标
  • 存在用户熟悉的设备/平台标准
    • 尽可能遵循这些标准图标以获得更好的用户体验
  • 何时使用自定义图标而不是标准图标
    • 使用自定义（非标准）图标来表示标准图标无法适应的应用程序特定功能而不会导致歧义（即食谱搜索、电影书签、图像下载）
      • 图标中的歧义可以解释为其他内容
    • 如果用户可能会犹豫或花费精力来找出一个图标，那么通常应该使用自定义图标
  • 如何确定您的图标是否不明确
    • 文本测试——确定你的图标是否有歧义
      • 如果图标需要标题或描述来传达其含义，您可能需要使用不同的图标或不使用图标
  • 图标上下文
    • 图标通过上下文获得清晰度和意义
    • 图标的放置方式和位置会影响它们的有效性
    • 布局影响清晰度
      • 太多的图标会让观众不知所措
      • 有时应该使用文本标签，即使它们可能需要通过语言翻译 - 特别是如果您不熟悉它们所传达/代表的内容
      • 对齐会影响图标的工作方式。 让文本标签左对齐-空白-右对齐标签为查看者创建了更多工作来来回扫描以链接元素
    • 文化影响意义
      • 北欧路标上的椒盐卷饼图标意味着感兴趣的地方，也意味着苹果键盘上的命令

图标可操作 + 状态 + 一览无余的解释

  • 导航
    • 快速找到机场中的位置（即带有箭头的洗手间图标表示左侧是浴室）
    • 帮助我们快速浏览元素（即扫描浴室图标的完整标志，不需要阅读整个标志）
    • 返回界面时帮助调用元素（即 Windows 95 开始菜单中的图标 + 文本）
  • 警告
    • 警告危险的路标上的图标（例如建筑、鹿过马路）
  • 触发动作
    • 当用作按钮时（即用于搜索的放大镜、用于录音的麦克风）
  • 显示状态
    • 可以显示二进制状态，如开/关
    • 可以显示远程状态，如音量、电池电量
    • 可以显示复合状态，例如显示电池电量的电池图标以及电池是否正在充电
    • 操作系统的一部分通常具有显示图标的状态集合（即三星设备通知栏）
  • 表达语调
    • 传达语气和表达品牌
    • 谷歌材料设计很简单，传达了功利主义的本质

谢谢！

如果您喜欢播客，请考虑给我们评论Apple Podcasts或查看我们的 Patreon 以在播客上大喊大叫。

在 Patreon 上支持我们

您可以在所有播客平台上找到我们，以及

推特（@htmleverything）
抖音



原文:

What is HTML All The Things?

HTML All The Things is a web development podcast and discord community which was started by Matt and Mike, developers based in Ontario, Canada.

The podcast speaks to web development topics as well as running a small business, self-employment and time management. You can join them for both their successes and their struggles as they try to manage expanding their Web Development business without stretching themselves too thin.

What's This One About?

This week Matt and Mike tackle the questions "What is iconography" and "What does iconography mean" in a packed episode that covers the basics through the UX of icons. The duo also covers a variety of iconography examples and when text should be used instead of icons, or when text & icons should be used together and why.

Show Notes

Iconography UX

  • “Iconography is a visual language used to represent features, functionality, or content. Icons are meant to be simple, visual elements that are recognized and understood immediately.”
  • Icons are universal (they don’t rely on a specific written/spoken language)
    • Generally this is true, a lot of people have different lived experiences and may not be familiar with some sort of object you’re trying to represent
  • Icons are visual metaphors
    • Speed up understanding by transferring properties from something that people already understand
    • Icons communicate to a viewer a metaphorical associations (ie envelope for mail)
    • The metaphor can be…
      • literal (ie alarm icon is an alarm clock)
      • outdated (ie save icon is a floppy disk)
      • Abstract (ie shuffle and refresh are arrows)
      • Original reference lost (ie Mac command icon)
  • Visually
    • Small symbol that represent an object, action, or idea
    • Usually a square 1:1 aspect ratio
    • If you do need to use custom icons, they should share:
      • Shared thematic style & consistent design elements
      • Icons should look like they are in a set or family
    • By having icons be visually consistent they become:
      • More aesthetically pleasing
      • Removes ambiguity
      • Helps users distinguish if an icon is interactive
    • Having consistent looking icons includes icons having the same height and fill (don’t mix filled and non filled icons). This prevents pulling the users eye disproportionately towards a particular icon
  • Device/platform standards exist that users are familiar with
    • Defer to these standard icons where possible for better UX
  • When to use custom icons instead of standard ones
    • Use custom (not standard) icons to rep app specific functionality that the standard icons can’t accommodate without causing ambiguity (ie recipe search, movie bookmark, image download)
      • Ambiguity as in the icon can be interpreted as something else
    • Beyond ambiguity custom icons should usually be used if the user is likely to hesitate or expend energy to figure out an icon
  • How to determine if you icon is ambiguous
    • The text test – determines if your icon is ambiguous
      • If icon needs title or description to convey its meaning, you may want to use a different icon or no icon
  • Icon Context
    • Icons gain clarity and meaning through context
    • How and where icons are placed impact how effective they are
    • Layout Impact Clarity
      • Too many icons can overwhelm a viewer
      • Sometimes text labels should be used instead even if they may need to be put through a language translator – especially if you aren’t familiar with what they’re conveying/representing
      • Alignment can impact how icons work. Having text label left aligned-white space-right aligned label creates more work for viewer to scan back and forth to link the elements
    • Culture Impacts Meaning
      • The pretzel icon on a Nordic road sign means place of interest, also means command on Apple keyboard

Icon Actionables + Status + Glanceable Explanations

  • Navigation
    • Quickly find places in an airport (ie washroom icon with an arrow left means bathrooms are on the left)
    • Help us quickly scan through elements (ie scan a full sign for the bathroom icon, don’t need to read entire sign)
    • Help recall elements when returning to an interface (ie icons + text in Windows 95 start menu)
  • Warnings
    • Icons on road signs that warn of dangers (ie construction, deer crossing)
  • Triggering Action
    • When used as a button (ie magnifying glass for search, microphone to record)
  • Showing status
    • Can show binary status like on/off
    • Can show ranged status like volume level, battery charge
    • Can show compound status like a battery icon that shows battery charge level AND if the battery is actively charging
    • There is typically a part of an OS with a collection of status showing icons (ie Samsung device notification shade)
  • Expressing Tone of Voice
    • Communicate tone and expressing brand
    • Firefox 2019 rebrand is bold, vibrant, and optimistic
    • Google Material Design is simple which conveys utilitarian nature

Thank you!

Items.Title: 穆菲朗 | Compiler in C not Rust | Mufi Lang
Items.Description:
我有一段时间没有写了，主要是因为我一直很忙，空闲时间都在写 Mufi-Lang。

我花了一些时间试图弄清楚用什么语言编写字节码编译器，我觉得用 C 编写它是最大的乐趣。一个原因，我没有太多 C 项目，第二，我学会了如何用 C 写一个编译器，三，为什么不呢？

不要以为我尝试过 Rust，我真的尝试过，但我的代码也保持原创性，坚持只使用标准库，我觉得 C 是我的闪亮骑士。 现在，我绝不是说 C 是更好的选择，或者 Rust 是其中之一，我给出了为什么我选择其中一个而不是另一个的懒惰原因，这些原因是基于舒适。

我对 Mufi 有很多计划，我计划首先使用 Python 设置脚本在 Unix 系统中发布，并且我必须弄清楚诸如 Winget、Snap 之类的东西。 我也会在 Markdown 中编写文档以开始使用，可能上面有一个简单的网站。

在0.1.0中，我预计不会有任何类型的导入系统，相反，您只需要依赖主要具有文件操作、数学函数等的标准库。

这种语言会逐渐发展，我确实希望有贡献者来扩展它的能力。 我知道它不会变大，但只是写它是一种兴奋。

查看repo以了解该项目的更多信息。



原文:

I haven't written in a while mostly because I've been busy, and spent my free time writing Mufi-Lang.

I spent some time trying to figure out what language to write the byte code compiler in, and I felt the biggest fun would be writing it in C. One reason, I don't have much projects in C, two, I learned how to write a compiler in C, and three, why the hell not?

Don't think I tried Rust, I really did, but too keep originality in my code, sticking with just standard library, I felt C was my shining knight. Now in no way am I saying C is a better choice, or Rust is one, I'm giving the lazy reasons of why I chose one over the other, and these reasons are based upon comfort.

I have a lot of plans for Mufi, I plan to first release in Unix systems using a Python setup script, and I'll have to figure out things like Winget, Snap and such. I will also write documentation in markdown to get started, probably have a simple website on it.

In 0.1.0 I do not anticipate of having any type of import system, and instead you'll just have to rely on the standard library that will mainly have file operations, math functions and such.

This language will grow gradually, and I do want to get contributors to expand it's capability. I know it's not going to go big, but just writing it is an euphoria.

The first release Baloo will be expected to release in the next month or two, and after that updates will come in a release cycle.

Check out the repo to see more of the project.

Items.Title: 如何让您的网站离线工作 | How to make your website work offline
Items.Description:
所以你已经建立了你的网站。 无论是 React 应用程序、Vue 应用程序还是一个好的旧 HTML 站点，都做得很好。 你应该为此感到自豪。 回想一下你写第一行代码的时候，现在你有了一个网站。 它是在线的，其他人可以看到它。

但是，该网站仅在您连接到 Internet 时才有效。 如果您的用户失去连接，那么他们将看到那个 chrome 恐龙而不是您可爱的网站。

那么你的解决方案是什么？ 你接受现状吗？ 离线网站甚至可能吗？
它的确是！ 渐进式 Web 应用程序 (PWA) 是您的解决方案。

什么是渐进式 Web 应用程序？

渐进式 Web 应用程序是可以像本机应用程序一样运行的 Web 应用程序。 使用 2 个文件，您的网站可以有推送通知、离线模式，并且可以像应用程序一样下载。 这两个文件称为服务工作者和清单 JSON 文件。

服务人员

你需要的第一个文件是一个服务工作者，它是一个 Javascript 文件，它是 PWA 的大脑。 您需要通过在基本 HTML 中添加脚本标记并添加此代码来注册它。

 if ('serviceWorker' in navigator) { navigator.serviceWorker.register('/service-worker.js') .then(function(registration) { console.log('Service worker started'); }) .catch(function(error) { console.log('Service worker failed, error:', error); }); }

在您单独service-worker.js文件中，您需要 2 块代码。

 self.addEventListener('install', (event) => { // run caching when installing service worker. }); self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => { // After install and all active pages have been closed, this will run. });

现在，您有了 PWA 的入门代码。 查找一些剩余代码以添加功能。

它包含有关网站的一些信息，例如名称、背景颜色和图标。 有关完整列表，请参见 MDN 上的此处。 该文件定义了一些 Web 应用程序元数据，例如下载时的网站图标。
将<link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.json">添加到根 HTML 文件的 head 元素中以将网站链接到它并将您的图标添加到图像文件夹中。

 { "short_name": "My Example PWA", "name": "Example PWA", "start_url": "/index.html", "background_color": "#1c5680", "display": "standalone", "theme_color": "#1c5680", "icons": [ { "src": "/images/icon.png", "type": "image/png", "sizes": "128x128" } ] }

制作离线页面

const custom_offline_page = "offline.html" self.addEventListener('install', event => { event.waitUntil( caches.open('CacheName').then((cache) => { return cache.addAll([ custom_offline_page ]) }) ) }) self.addEventListener("fetch", (event) => { event.respondWith( (async () => { try { // Fetch request from network const networkResponse = await fetch(event.request) return networkResponse } catch (error) { // Error thrown when a user has no internet connection const cache = await caches.open('CacheName') const cachedResponse = await cache.match(custom_offline_page) return cachedResponse } })() ) })

你现在有一个离线页面。

测试它是否有效

在 chrome 中，打开开发工具并转到灯塔选项卡。 您可以运行审核。 勾选框之一是 PWA。 运行审核，如果您有任何问题，这将揭示任何问题。

您还可以检查开发工具中的application选项卡以查看清单 json 并查看服务工作者是否已成功注册。

如果您有任何疑问或问题，请将它们放在下面的评论中。 我很乐意提供帮助

原文:

So you've build your website. Whether that is a React app, a Vue app or a good old HTML site, well done. You should be proud of it. Think back to when you wrote your first line of code, now you have a website. It's online and other people can see it.

However the website only works when you are connected to the Internet. If your users lose connection then they will see that chrome dinosaur instead of your lovely site.

So what's your solution? Do you accept the status quo? Is an offline website even possible?
Indeed it is! Progressive Web Apps(PWAs) are your solution.

What are Progressive Web Apps?

Progressive Web apps are Web applications that can act like native apps. Using 2 files, your website can have push notifications, offline mode and become downloadable like an app. These 2 files are called a service worker and a manifest JSON file.

Service worker

The first file you need is a service worker which is a Javascript file that is the brains of the PWA. You'll need to register it by adding a script tag in your base HTML and adding this code.

    if ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {
      navigator.serviceWorker.register('/service-worker.js')
        .then(function(registration) {
          console.log('Service worker started');
        })
        .catch(function(error) {
          console.log('Service worker failed, error:', error);
        });
    }

In your seperate service-worker.js file, you'll need 2 blocks of code.

self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
    // run caching when installing service worker.
});

self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
  // After install and all active pages have been closed, this will run.
});

Now you have a starter code for your PWA. Look up some of the remaining code to add the functionality.

Manjest.json

The manifest.json file is similar to a package.json file in a node project. It contains some information about the website like name, background color and icon. For the full list see here on MDN. This file defines some of the Web apps meta data such as what the website icon will be when you download it.
Add <link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.json"> into the head element of the root HTML file to link the website to it and add your icon to an images folder.

{
    "short_name": "My Example PWA",
    "name": "Example PWA",
    "start_url": "/index.html",
    "background_color": "#1c5680",
    "display": "standalone",
    "theme_color": "#1c5680",
    "icons": [
       {
           "src": "/images/icon.png",
           "type": "image/png",
           "sizes": "128x128"
       }
     ]
}

Making an offline page 

const custom_offline_page = "offline.html"

self.addEventListener('install', event => {
  event.waitUntil(
    caches.open('CacheName').then((cache) => {
      return cache.addAll([
        custom_offline_page
      ])
    })
  )
})

self.addEventListener("fetch", (event) => {
  event.respondWith(
    (async () => {
      try {
        // Fetch request from network
        const networkResponse = await fetch(event.request)
        return networkResponse
      } catch (error) {
        // Error thrown when a user has no internet connection
        const cache = await caches.open('CacheName')
        const cachedResponse = await cache.match(custom_offline_page)
        return cachedResponse
      }
    })()
  )
})

And you now have an offline page.

Testing it works

In chrome, open up the dev tools and go to the lighthouse tab. You can run an audit. One of the tick boxes is PWA. Run the audit and this will reveal any problems if you have any.

You can also check the application tab in dev tools to see the manifest json and to see if a service worker has successfully been registered.

If you have any questions or issues, drop them in the comments below. I'd be more than happy to help

Items.Title: 将 sudo 添加到 Windows | Adding sudo to windows
Items.Description:
在具有提升权限的窗口中打开终端有时很烦人，因为您必须依靠某种形式的 GUI 来选择Run as Admin选项。 在 Linux 中，我们可以使用热键打开终端，然后在同一终端使用sudo直接获得 root 访问权限。

在 Windows 中，有一个名为gsudo的工具，它允许我们将普通的 cmd 或 powershell 终端变成管理终端，就像sudo在 Linux 中所做的那样。 因此，我们只需在gsudo中添加我们喜欢运行的任何命令，它就会向我们显示一个用户访问控制 (UAC) 弹出窗口，我们可以在其中插入我们的用户名和密码。 用法

为了让gsudo在 Linux 中的工作更像sudo ，我们可以启用凭据缓存，这将显示更少的 UAC 弹出窗口，就像在 Linux 中一样，默认情况下sudo会记住您的密码 15 分钟。

 gsudo config CacheMode Auto


原文:

It is sometimes annoying to open a terminal in windows with elevated privileges as you have to rely on some form of GUI to select the option Run as Admin. In Linux we can just open the terminal with a hotkey and then get root access straight with sudo from the same terminal.

In windows, there's a tool called gsudo that allows us to turn our normal cmd or powershell terminal into an administrative terminal just like sudo does in Linux. So, we just prepend whatever command we like to run with gsudo and it will show us a User Access Control(UAC) pop-up where we can insert our username and password. Usage

To make gsudo work more like sudo in linux, we can enable credentials cache that will show less UAC popups just like in Linux where sudo remembers your password for 15 minutes by default.

gsudo config CacheMode Auto

Items.Description:
政府 私营部门计划和执行的公共工程 首先我想强调一些关键概念 1. 一个部落的所有男人和女人最初都必须知道如何执行所有维持生计的任务，这随着成员的任务专业化而发展在部落中，每个成员都是某个任务时间的专家，并且该任务的专家执行该任务而不是其他人，一旦完成了该群体生存所需的所有任务，该部落的其他成员将执行其他不同的任务, 所产生的资源在群体中的分配与群体中每个人的贡献成正比 评估某人对一般生产任务的贡献程度的方式是通过人们在其中的所有评价或偏好的总和和平均值来完成的部落有这两个关键概念 叫分工和主观价值论 部落进化了如何改善财富分配作为一个整体 5 因为互联网是作为有机体或个人一部分的男性和女性之间的连接机制 互联网是我们的神经系统所基于的结构之一 6 神经系统是我们适应环境的一种机制环境 众所周知，具有更好神经系统的动物是更进化的动物，能够更好地适应环境 从那里我们得出结论，有些事情我们不能没有复杂的神经系统 比较没有中枢神经系统的动物和那些这样做的人，或多或少简而言之，那些只有周围神经系统的人可以从环境中获取信息并感受，但不能管理信息并从中学习和做出决定 7 互联网是我们神经系统的基本基础，没有它我们就像没有中枢神经系统的动物区块链和大数据是更复杂的机制，可以改善我们的神经系统，为什么我们有统治者？ 我们的系统通过民主运作，所有做出的决定都是民主运作的，但有两种民主类型：代议制和参与性。 当我们说所有决策都通过民主进行时，这意味着尽管公民不决定政府中的问题，但他们可以。 他们选择的代表，但是因为使用代表而不是直接决定重要问题的人，因为在所有人之间决定重要问题实际上是不可能的，因为有大量的人，这随着区块链技术和大数据具有透明的选票收集系统和读取和轻松管理选票的系统，可以由人民创建政府，任何公民都可以提出任何需要公民合作的请求，将有一个系统将其分发到整个网络那个要求或提案以及那些坚持它的人将签署它，它就像一个签名集合，将会发生的是，将有非常相似的提案可以在作者之间进行修改以达成一个单一的提案在提出类似建议的人的共识下，如果没有达成共识，那么这些建议将被视为两个不同的建议，并且 eac 在现任政府中，根据可用的预算以及在以前的提案上花费了多少来决定必须有多少提案以及有更多或更少的提案。 在此模型中，决定了在给定时间段内必须解决的提案数量。 也就是比如下个月有5个问题，通过一次投票，决定有多少问题进入最后的投票，拿下所有的票，取所有票的平均值，这个平均值决定有多少个提案将被处理，从什么日期到什么日期再次完成该过程，即重新投票等，然后公共工程系统如何运作，公民将如何处理这些提案这些提案将赢得选票在最初的投票或签名收集中，公民将阅读这些提案，并考虑到哪一个的签名最多，他们将有一个标准，该标准由多少提案组成，他们有多少签名，然后形成哪个提案更紧迫的标准然后将形成一个标准，即有多少问题必须进入最终投票，然后将有多少问题必须进入，而不是哪些问题（也就是说，不是什么提案投票最多的提案将进入投票，例如，如果决定在下个月必须处理五个提案，则前 5 个投票最多的提案将进入投票，计票将重新开始，并进行新的选举举行。 得票最多的将是当月进行的。 进入项目的下一阶段，这将确定开始收集过程的最终价格，没有人有义务参与其中。 要确定工作的最终成本，需要与网络中所有想要参与该过程的人一起进行审计。 如果你想建一座桥，网络中的所有参与者都会说需要什么，然后取平均值，所有需要的东西的平均值就是购买的东西。 所有想要参与说需要它的人都必须支付少量费用，以确保他们这样做不是出于恶意或在不知情的情况下发表意见，所以如果你在不知情的情况下发表意见，以及网络意识到，他们会投票，看看大多数人是否在不知道的情况下考虑了你的想法，或者如果大多数人认为你的想法知道是的，大多数人认为你在不知道的情况下认为该金额将被没收，并将被抽奖另一方面，网络的所有成员以及获得它的人都将获得该金额，如果人们在结束作品估价过程后相信您所知道的，则该金额将退还给您。 拥有工作中需要的产品或服务的人，并希望发布他们的产品成本（即价格）多少的建议，信息将到达整个网络。 但这并不止于此，当我们决定购买什么服务或产品时，我们看到有两个因素需要考虑价格和质量 我们已经知道我们将如何获得服务或产品的价格，但是如何我们是否确定它必须是什么质量质量价格比质量是由市场决定的供求规律这意味着人是决定质量的人那么我们将采取人决定质量的公理来制作一个投票选出他们认为最多的产品质量更高的产品 您将存入一笔金额，以保证没有恶意或人们在不知情的情况下发表意见 一旦进行投票，产品的质量指数将是投票给它的人数，当它已经决定哪个是第一个产品时，对第二个和第三个重复该过程，这样价格质量关系就会将根据可用信息来决定人们将能够投票选择哪些产品将用于工作，这并不总是必须是最物有所值的产品，而是公民通过投票选择的产品。 这次不会扣留任何金额。 完成工作所需的所有组件的总和被犁出，这就是工作成本的确定方式，一旦确定成本，它将转嫁给所有希望为工作做出贡献的公民的集合，如果我不知道如何收集足够的工作被认为是不可行的，并且重复之前的过程，直到工作被认为是可行的。 该工作必须包括一小部分紧急费用 该百分比将进行表决，以便在收到钱后决定紧急百分比 将与产品和服务的所有者签订智能合约，其中包括体力劳动的智力工作者材料产品和服务这个问题可以外推到政府的所有问题随着这个系统的演进我们将不再需要像总统和部长这样的中央权力机构的完美状态。 完美的国家是最低限度的，也就是说，它的所有结构都只是安全和正义，即警察和正义，没有税收制度，如何实现所有人的安全和正义？ 有一句话我喜欢，是的，我们都是超级英雄，没有人是如果我们推断这个概念并说是的，我们都很强大，没有人是，也就是说，是的，我们都很强大，没有人集中力量如何我们是否知道某些团体通过金钱集中力量购买正义，然后我们认为正义能够实现分散的正义和安全系统。 它将以这种方式实现。 我们为什么要教育自己？ 我们为什么要上学？ 对于快乐不清楚，很明显有快乐的知识，人们可以为了快乐而学习，但快乐被抛在脑后，普遍的是义务，为什么必须学习？ 为什么根据法律我们都必须这样做？ 这只有一个简单的答案，让我们成为有更多机会过上体面生活的公民，而不是被强权操纵，但当前的教育远未满足这两个前提，而是它所做的只是操纵我们以支持强权，这个有解决办法。 值对象

价值对象 são como o nome diz, um objeto que não tem operações associadas, ele serve somente para guardar valores, no exemplo acima o Address é um value object pois quando uma pessoa muda para a rua ao lado, mesmo que a unica parte que tenha mudado no endereço dela seja a rua, nós não falamos que ela ATUALIZOU a rua no endereço, e sim que ela MUDOU de endereço, o que no sistema se traduziria em um novo value object e não em um update dentro de um value object já existente

    Nessa classe de order temos um método para retornar o total dá order em especifico mas e se quisermos calcular o total de todas as orders? Nessa classe de order temos um método para retornar o total dá order em especifico mas e se quisermos calcular o total de todas as orders？ Não faz sentido ficar como método de uma order já q esse método vai precisar receber outras orders para fazer a conta, é nesse caso que devemos criar o service de Orders Não faz sentido ficar como método de uma order já q esse método vai precisar receber outras orders para fazer a conta, é nesse caso que devemos criar o service de Orders

    O OrderService pode conter todas os métodos que não fazem sentido ficarem dentro de Order inclusive por exemplo criar uma nova order O OrderService pode conter todas os métodos que não fazem sentido ficarem dentro de Order inclusive por exemplo criar uma nova order

原文:
  • Aggregates
  • Value Objects
  • Services
  • Implementação

Um Aggregate é um padrão em DDD onde temos um conjuntos de objetos de dominio e value objects podem ser tratados como uma unidade

Value Objects

Value objects são como o nome diz, um objeto que não tem operações associadas, ele serve somente para guardar valores, no exemplo acima o Address é um value object pois quando uma pessoa muda para a rua ao lado, mesmo que a unica parte que tenha mudado no endereço dela seja a rua, nós não falamos que ela ATUALIZOU a rua no endereço, e sim que ela MUDOU de endereço, o que no sistema se traduziria em um novo value object e não em um update dentro de um value object já existente

Services

Quando falamos em DDD o Service é uma operação sem estado que cumpre uma tarefa especifica do domínio. A melhor indicação que devemos criar um service é quando queremos fazer uma operação que não parece fazer sentido como o método em um Agreggate ou um Value Object.

Cuidados
  • Caso existam muitos services no projeto isso pode indicar que os aggregates estão anêmicos
  • Services são Stateless, O que significa que eles não carregam valores de uma chamada para a outra

Implementação

Para o mesmo sistema demonstrado acima podemos ter por exemplo uma entidade de orders que contem items como value object

export class Order{ private _id: string; private _customerId: string; private _items: OrderItem[]; private _total: number; constructor(id: string, customerId: string, items: OrderItem[]){ this._id = id; this._customerId = customerId; this._items = items; this._total = this.total(); } total(): number{ return this._items.reduce((acc, item) => acc + item.price, 0) } }

Nessa classe de order temos um método para retornar o total dá order em especifico mas e se quisermos calcular o total de todas as orders? Não faz sentido ficar como método de uma order já q esse método vai precisar receber outras orders para fazer a conta, é nesse caso que devemos criar o service de Orders

export class OrderService{ static total(orders: Order[]): number { return orders.reduce((acc, order) => acc + order.total(), 0); }
}

O OrderService pode conter todas os métodos que não fazem sentido ficarem dentro de Order inclusive por exemplo criar uma nova order

export class OrderService{ static total(orders: Order[]): number { return orders.reduce((acc, order) => acc + order.total(), 0); } static placeOrder(customer: Customer, items: OrderItem[]): Order{ if(items.length === 0){ throw new Error("Order must have at least one item") } const order = new Order(uuid(), customer.id, items); return order }
}

Ainda estou aprendendo sobre DDD e se quiser ver melhor o código pode dar uma olhada nesse Repositório
